articleTitle: Indiana Jones et le Temple du Péril
introSection: Indiana Jones et le Temple du Péril (English: Indiana Jones and the Temple of Peril) is a roller coaster attraction at Disneyland Paris. It opened on 30 July 1993. Based on the Indiana Jones films, guests are taken on an adventure riding in a mining train through a lost temple. The attraction was sponsored by Esso.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Indiana Jones et le Temple du Péril opened on 30 July 1993, becoming the first Disney roller coaster to have an inversion.

The ride ran backwards from 2000 to 2004.

In 2014, the ride was refurbished, including a partial retracking.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: The ride's layout is actually the same as the Pinfari built TL-59 model. Intamin did change the track and trains to feature their own, however.

fullText:
Indiana Jones et le Temple du Péril (English: Indiana Jones and the Temple of Peril) is a roller coaster attraction at Disneyland Paris. It opened on 30 July 1993. Based on the Indiana Jones films, guests are taken on an adventure riding in a mining train through a lost temple. The attraction was sponsored by Esso.

Indiana Jones et le Temple du Péril opened on 30 July 1993, becoming the first Disney roller coaster to have an inversion.

The ride ran backwards from 2000 to 2004.

In 2014, the ride was refurbished, including a partial retracking.

The ride's layout is actually the same as the Pinfari built TL-59 model. Intamin did change the track and trains to feature their own, however.

Brown track and supports.
